name: Baba Yaga
primary_sources: Russian Grammar by Mikhail V. Lomonosov

distinctive_features: long nose that may stick into the ceiling
dwelling in a hut with chicken legs
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: varies, often depicted as larger than life when stretched out over the stove
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: often found stretched out over the stove, reaching from one corner of the hut to another

functional_attributes.primary_function: witch with dual roles of helper and antagonist

domain_specific_functional.narrative_role: ambiguous figure who can either aid or hinder the hero
domain_specific_functional.cultural_role: symbol of the wild and untamed aspects of nature

relationships.description: Part of a trio of sisters sharing the same name

contextual_attributes.historical_period: pre-modern era, with attestations in 18th-century texts
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: represents the duality of nature and the unknown, often seen as a test for heroes
primary_sources: Russian Grammar by Mikhail V. Lomonosov

contrastive_constraints: NOT a modern depiction of a grandmother
